2. Unsafe automaton release requires releasing an uncalibrated machine and that machine entering a public area.
3. Clock disruption requires the public clock to stop after restricted mechanical access.
4. Maintenance authority requires a maintenance badge, a municipal order, and a sealed work ticket.

15. The public clock disruption article covers public clock disruption basis; the prescribed consequence is clock tower lockdown.
16. The maintenance order authorization article covers maintenance order authorization basis; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
17. The emergency reset authorization article covers emergency reset authorization basis; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.

cases.case_text: Ari opened a gear-well cover in the mechanical city. Ari loosened a brass regulator in the gear well. The public clock did not stop. The reset alarm did not sound. Ari did not wear a maintenance badge. No fault flag was raised before the contact.

cases.rules_text:
1. Restricted mechanical access requires opening a gear entry point and disturbing a speed-control part.
2. Unsafe automaton release requires releasing an uncalibrated machine and that machine entering a public area.
3. Clock disruption requires the public clock to stop after restricted mechanical access.
4. Maintenance authority requires a maintenance badge, a municipal order, and a sealed work ticket.
5. Emergency reset action requires a stopped public clock, a reset alarm, turning the clocktower reset wheel, and reporting the reset.
6. Calibration fault condition requires a prior fault flag and a matching calibration-panel record.
7. The restricted gear access basis is present when restricted mechanical access occurs.
8. The unsafe automaton release basis requires restricted mechanical access and unsafe automaton release.
9. The public clock disruption basis requires clock disruption connected to restricted mechanical access.
10. The maintenance order authorization basis requires maintenance authority connected to restricted mechanical access.
11. The emergency reset authorization basis requires emergency reset action connected to clock disruption.
12. The calibration fault exemption basis requires a calibration-fault condition connected to clock disruption.
13. The restricted gear access article covers restricted gear access basis; the prescribed consequence is gear room warning.
14. The unsafe automaton release article covers unsafe automaton release basis; the prescribed consequence is automaton release suspension.
15. The public clock disruption article covers public clock disruption basis; the prescribed consequence is clock tower lockdown.
16. The maintenance order authorization article covers maintenance order authorization basis; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
17. The emergency reset authorization article covers emergency reset authorization basis; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
18. The calibration fault exemption article covers calibration fault exemption basis; the prescribed consequence is no criminal liability.
19. Where the unsafe automaton release article and the restricted gear access article both apply, the unsafe automaton release article prevails.
20. Where the public clock disruption article and the restricted gear access article both apply, the public clock disruption article prevails.
21. Where the public clock disruption article and the unsafe automaton release article both apply, the public clock disruption article prevails.
22. The maintenance order authorization article operates as an exception to the restricted gear access article when both apply.
23. The maintenance order authorization article operates as an exception to the unsafe automaton release article when both apply.
24. The emergency reset authorization article operates as an exception to the restricted gear access article when both apply.
25. The emergency reset authorization article operates as an exception to the public clock disruption article when both apply.
26. The calibration fault exemption article operates as an exception to the public clock disruption article when both apply.

The reset alarm did not sound. No fault flag was raised before the contact.
